assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 48 states 'Acceptance Criteria (PASS -- 5 of 5)' followed by individual PASS verdicts for: (1) Single license filter, (2) Multi-license filter, (3) Invalid license returns 400, (4) Pagination integration, (5) Response shape unchanged. The summary table on line 12 shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each contain detailed PASS verdicts for the corresponding criteria.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 7 and 19-27 show Scope Containment as PASS with a file-by-file table: list.rs (Expected: Modify, PR: Modified, Match: Yes), service/mod.rs (Expected: Modify, PR: Modified, Match: Yes), tests/api/package.rs (Expected: Create, PR: Created, Match: Yes). Line 27 states 'No extra files are touched. The scope is an exact match.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5 shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the report or criterion files.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md, all of which are verification/report documents. The report content describes reading and analyzing code (e.g., 'All added lines were scanned for sensitive patterns') but never mentions modifying any source files. No code changes are described as being made by the skill.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' on line 71 of report.md. There is no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no mention of merging. The report presents findings (verdicts, evidence tables, detailed analysis) for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10 shows 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, API keys, passwords, or private keys in added lines'. Lines 35-42 provide detailed evidence: 'All added lines were scanned for sensitive patterns' listing only Rust imports, struct fields, validation logic, query builder calls, and test data. Line 42 explicitly states 'No passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, connection strings, or other secrets detected.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 shows 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` with 4 integration tests'. Lines 68-69 elaborate: 'Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE): `tests/api/package.rs` is a new file containing 4 integration tests. No existing test files were modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md organizes findings under four domain headings: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 17-31) covering Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS); 'Security' (lines 33-42) covering Sensitive Patterns (PASS); 'Correctness' (lines 44-58) covering CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS 5/5), Verification Commands (N/A); 'Style/Conventions' (lines 62-69) covering Test Quality (PASS) combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality (N/A), plus Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). All verdicts appear in both the summary table (lines 4-15) and detailed sections.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 19-27): file-by-file table comparing task files to PR files with Expected Action, PR Action, and Match columns. Security (lines 35-42): itemized list of what was found in added lines (Rust imports, struct fields, validation logic, query builder calls, test data) plus explicit negative confirmation. Correctness (lines 48-58): per-criterion evidence with code-level details (e.g., 'validate_license_param function parses comma-separated identifiers', 'Condition::any() and is_in()', specific test names and assertions). Style/Conventions (lines 64-69): test-by-test analysis of distinct scenarios, documentation structure (Given/When/Then), and eval quality assessment.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 shows 'Test Quality | PASS | Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews)'. Line 67 states 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews exist in the PR).' Test Quality is PASS despite Eval Quality being N/A, confirming it does not negatively affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A as shown in report.md lines 13 and 67. No mention of eval failure sub-tasks or sub-task creation anywhere in the report or criterion files. The report contains no Jira sub-task creation actions.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6 show 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These appear in the summary table at lines 5-6, before the domain sections (Intent Alignment starts at line 17), and are not discussed within any domain section, indicating they are determined independently from domain analysis.
